title: F17d Paradox Resolution: pow = 1n for Positive Domain via EPL/ELMl
author: Arturo R. Almaguer
status: RESOLVED â€” inconsistency corrected in superbest_v5_table.json and superbest.py

paradox_statement.observation: EPL(x,y) = exp(x*ln(y)) = y^x appears in Sixteen_Operator_Census.tex as one of the 16 F16 operators (ELMl, Class I). If ELMl is in F16, then pow(x,n) = x^n = EPL(n,x) should cost 1 node, not 3.
paradox_statement.contradiction: 3-node construction ignores the direct EPL primitive, which is already a member of F16.

resolution.answer: EPL(x,y) = exp(x*ln(y)) = y^x IS in F16. pow(x,n) = x^n = EPL(n,x) for x > 0 is a 1-node F16 application.
resolution.explanation: The 3-node construction EXL(0,n) â†’ EXL(result,x) â†’ EML(result,1) was an expanded decomposition. It failed to recognize EPL as a usable primitive operator. Once EPL/ELMl is acknowledged as an active F16 operator, the construction collapses to a single node.
resolution.domain_condition: x > 0 is required because EPL(n,x) = exp(n*ln(x)) contains ln(x). For x <= 0, ln is undefined in real F16. There is no real F16 tree for pow(x,n) when x can be negative.
resolution.general_domain_cost: For general real x (possibly negative), the best known cost remains 3n using the expanded EXL+ELAd+EML route â€” but this also requires x > 0 for the ln step. For truly general x (negative base), no finite real F16 tree exists for non-integer n.
resolution.integer_exponent_note: For integer n, pow(x,n) can be handled via repeated mul, but mul itself is domain-restricted to x > 0 in real F16.

pow.construction_positive: EPL(n,x) = ELMl(n,x) = exp(n*ln(x)) = x^n â€” single F16 node
pow.construction_general: eml(exl(exl(0,n),x), 1) â€” 3-node expanded construction (x > 0 still required)
pow.domain_general: x > 0 required (ln constraint); same as positive for practical purposes

positive_domain_table_v5_1.description: Costs for all 10 operations when inputs are positive reals (x > 0, y > 0)

general_domain_table_v5_1.description: Costs for operations under general real-domain assumptions

entries.construction: NO single real F16 tree for all x,y

excluded: mul â€” NO_F16_TREE for general signed multiplication

headline_numbers.improvement_from_X20: positive domain drops from 18n to 16n; savings 75.3% â†’ 78.1%
headline_numbers.v5_1_note: The 78.1% headline applies to the positive domain. For equations mixing positive and negative values, the general-domain 18n figure (v5) applies to most ops except pow where positive-base can still use 1n.

downstream_updates_needed.description: Any result file or catalog that records pow cost = 3n for positive-base equations should be updated to 1n per operation under positive-domain assumption.
affected_categories: Equations in the physics catalog where the base x is always positive (mass, length, energy, temperature, probability, density, etc.) and contain pow(x,n): each pow occurrence drops from 3n to 1n.
Any SuperBEST cost table entries that cited pow = 3n without noting positive-domain correction.
Linear cost law: if an equation uses pow k times with positive base, total cost drops by 2k nodes.
Savings percentage for such equations increases by roughly 2k/naive_total per equation.
specific_files_to_check: python/results/s93_sb_counterexample.json â€” check if pow is used; if so, update cost
python/results/x8_domain_audit_table.json â€” pow audit entry (3n) correct for expanded construction but should note EPL=1n
python/results/x7_mul_domain_audit.json â€” pow domain entry notes 'unknown', should note x > 0 AND EPL route
Any catalog equation files listing pow(x,n) cost = 3n in positive context
downstream_updates_needed.priority: Medium â€” the v5 table total (18n) was the advertised headline; it should now be footnoted as 'general domain'. Positive domain (16n = 78.1%) is the new stronger headline for physics applications.
downstream_updates_needed.note_on_s93: s93_sb_counterexample.json is in git diff (modified). Check if it references pow cost in a positive-domain context.

mathematical_note.EPL_is_ELMl: EPL(x,y) = exp(x*ln(y)) = y^x. ELMl in Sixteen_Operator_Census.tex (Class I, combining op h = exponentiation). This is a genuine F16 operator â€” it is one of the 16 binary compositions of {exp, -exp, ln} with {+, -, *, /} restricted to outputs of the form exp(g(x,y)) or ln(g(x,y)).
mathematical_note.why_not_recognized_in_v5: SuperBEST v5 used a curated 8-operator subset {EML, DEML, EXL, EAL, LEAd, ELAd, ELSb, LEdiv}. ELMl/EPL was in the census but not in the operational lookup. This is now corrected: EPL is the direct 1-node operator for pow(x,n) = x^n when x > 0.
mathematical_note.F16_completeness_preserved: Adding EPL to the active set does not change which functions are representable (it was already in ELC). It only reduces the cost of pow from 3 to 1 for the positive domain.
mathematical_note.lower_bound_still_valid: The 3-node lower bound in v5 was proved for the expanded-tree model. It remains valid if EPL is excluded. Once EPL is included, the lower bound is trivially 1 (a single leaf node call). No contradiction â€” the lower bound proof assumed a specific operator set.
mathematical_note.X20_verdict: The F17d paradox is resolved: there is no F17 needed. EPL is already in F16; pow = 1n for positive domain within F16 itself.
